label: shadow-flip-verify-2026-05-16
summary: Phase 4.5 dual-fire wire verified end-to-end. V1 main now ships fireShadow() and Vercel prod has VITE_V2_SHADOW_URL set. V2 lives at its own Vercel project (california-law-chatbot-v2). Shadow handler was patched with waitUntil() — without it, Vercel serverless killed the async agent loop before it could write the KV record. After the patch, smoke probe wrote the full shadow:* record plus an audit_record_envelope:* envelope-encrypted entry plus an audit:YYYY-MM-DD daily list entry.

topology_change.before: V1 and V2 shared one Vercel project (prj_1RE4M9EIZDM4XibdnQtxku78j8dB). V2 only deployed to previews.
topology_change.after: V1 stays on the original project. V2 lives on new project prj_1T0oQriIdbO2BlX81wSg2L1i1AzL aliased to https://california-law-chatbot-v2.vercel.app
topology_change.decision_recorded_via: AskUserQuestion 2026-05-16 — option 'New Vercel project for V2' selected

V2_prod_initial.issue: shadow handler used `void runShadow(body)` — fired-and-forgotten — Vercel reclaimed the instance before agent loop could complete; zero KV writes.

V2_prod_fixed.patch: Wrapped runShadow in waitUntil() from @vercel/functions; added @vercel/functions dep.

request.user_text: What is California Code of Civil Procedure section 425.16?

remaining_gates_before_phase5: Malpractice carrier UPL written confirmation (Arjun, external)
GLiNER daemon install on each attorney laptop (one-time, scripts/install-daemon.sh)
7 consecutive clean days of shadow-run-report.mjs (≤20% material divergence, zero V2 critical hallucinations)
